response.reasoning:
The static analysis has produced a single group (group_1) containing 4 files: the orders route, inventory service, payment service, and product repository. Let me assess whether this grouping is optimal.

Looking at the call graph:
- src/routes/orders.ts calls both inventoryService.updateInventory and paymentService.processPayment
- Both inventoryService and paymentService call productRepo.findProduct

This is a classic "diamond" dependency pattern (hence the branch name "feature/diamond"), where the route fans out to two services that both converge on the same repository. All four files are part of the same logical flow: placing an order triggers inventory and payment processing, both of which need to look up products.

The grouping is coherent and correctly represents a single end-to-end order processing flow. There are no ungrouped files, no unrelated changes mixed in, and the review order (repository → services → route, bottom-up by flow_position) makes semantic sense.

No refinements are needed here.
